title: Death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications
subtitle: The estimated death rate from [neonatal](#dod:neonatal) [preterm birth complications](#dod:preterm-birth-complications) in children under five years old, per 100,000.
caption:
Death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications, 2023
The estimated death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications in children under five years old, per 100,000.

This choropleth map displays the estimated death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications for children under five years old in 2023, measured per 100,000 population. The visualization uses a continuous color scale ranging from pale yellow for the lowest rates to deep navy blue for the highest rates, with specific legend bands marking thresholds at 0, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500, and 1,000 deaths. The geographic distribution shows the highest mortality rates concentrated across sub-Saharan Africa, where countries such as Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Somalia fall into the darkest blue bands representing rates between 200 and 1,000 per 100,000. In contrast, the lowest rates appear across Western and Northern Europe, including Norway, Sweden, and Iceland, as well as Australia and Japan, where the color falls in the pale yellow band corresponding to values between 0 and 10 per 100,000. North America and South America display intermediate values; the United States and Canada sit in the light teal band around 20 to 50, while parts of South America like Brazil and Argentina show slightly lower rates in the 10 to 20 range. Large portions of Asia show varied patterns, with India and Pakistan appearing in medium blue shades indicating rates between 50 and 200, while China and Russia appear in lighter greens and yellows indicating lower burdens. The data shows a clear global disparity where the highest mortality burdens cluster in specific regions of Africa and South Asia, while the lowest burdens cluster in high-income regions of Europe, Oceania, and East Asia.
